entities.name: FARM INCOME GROWTH
entities.description: The increase in income generated by farms over a specific period.

entities.name: INEQUALITY
entities.description: The disparity in income distribution among farm populations.

entities.name: POVERTY REDUCTION
entities.description: The process of reducing the number of people living in poverty.

entities.name: SMALLHOLDER IRRIGATION SYSTEMS
entities.description: Agricultural systems where small-scale farmers use irrigation for crop production.

entities.name: ZIMBABWE
entities.description: A country in Southern Africa where the study was conducted.

entities.name: TANZANIA
entities.description: A country in East Africa where the study was conducted.

entities.name: MOZAMBIQUE
entities.description: A country in Southeast Africa where the study was conducted.

entities.name: EMPIRICAL ANALYSIS
entities.description: A research approach based on observed and measured phenomena and derivatives.

relationships.description: Mechanisms link growth and inequality, where low growth increases relative inequality and high growth reduces it.
Growth mechanisms are critical for achieving poverty reduction.
Inequality reduction is a goal linked to poverty reduction strategies.
The study uses empirical analysis to evaluate farm income growth.
The study uses empirical analysis to evaluate changes in inequality.
Smallholder irrigation communities are located in Zimbabwe.
Smallholder irrigation communities are located in Tanzania.
Smallholder irrigation communities are located in Mozambique.
Farm income growth is evaluated within smallholder irrigation systems.
Inequality is evaluated within smallholder irrigation systems.
